summary: Persist-repoint EQUIVALENT: inline flush == writeChanged (partition/CASE/chunk/sort/otherUpdates identical, P1+S5 only deltas). trx-bound, retry-safe, no scheduleQueue, no side-effect loss. No BLOCK/WARN.

findings.claim: slack_mins not emitted as a CASE column in the batched path
findings.evidence: writeChanged batched updateFields has no slack_mins CASE; legacy inline flush (deleted runSchedule.js ~1714-1773) also omitted slack_mins from the batched updateFields — byte-faithful. A row with slack_mins+scheduled_at loses slack_mins in BOTH old and new code.
findings.fix: No change for W3 (equivalence preserved). If the legacy drop is itself a latent data bug, refer to elmo / backlog.

findings.claim: reconcile safety-net write uses db (not trx) with clockNow() for updated_at
findings.evidence: Line 886 db('task_instances')...update({unscheduled:1, updated_at:_runScheduleCommand.clockNow()}) — deliberate survives-rollback path (comment 884-885); was db.fn.now(), now JS Date (P1-consistent). Not routed through the command. Rollback/data-integrity semantics of the dual-connection write are elmo's column.

findings.claim: phase-1 chunk INSERT stamps created_at/updated_at with clockNow() and projects ISO for rowToTask
findings.evidence: Lines 1006-1037: DB row gets JS Date (P1), phase1InsertedById projection uses new Date().toISOString() so rowToTask can parse. Equivalence reasoning sound; golden-master pins changeset output.
findings.fix: REFER->zoe to confirm golden-master covers phase-1 insert changeset projection.

findings.claim: write-set equivalence (S5) is what gates the leg; needs adversarial verification of write-set assertions
findings.evidence: Refactor gate (goldenMaster.h6 + scheduleAdapters.contract) present. Repoint equivalence depends on the WRITE set, not just golden output — test truthfulness of write-set assertions is zoe's column.

refers.why: P1/data-integrity/sync co-lead: dual-connection safety-net write (runSchedule.js:886) + slack_mins batched-drop latent quirk
test truthfulness of write-set/golden-master equivalence and phase-1 insert changeset projection coverage
non-trivial refactor: design-time Scooter consult block + slice topology/boundary depth (ARCH-REVIEW.md)
